title: $ $ Contains the Supremum $ $ Was Missing
statement:
Let
\[
S=\{q\in\mathbb{Q}:0_{\mathbb{Q}}<q\land q\cdot q<2_{\mathbb{Q}}\}.
\]
Then
\[
\sqrt{2}=\sup \iota(S).
\]
Thus the bounded-above rational set with no rational supremum has a supremum
in $\mathbb{R}$.

current_dependencies.title: The Square-Two Gap Has No Rational Supremum
